author: Eurogamer
text:
Dragon's Dogma 2 discontinues deluxe edition - and withdraws many of its controversial time-saving microtransactions

Capcom is discontinuing the deluxe edition of Dragon's Dogma 2 on 24th June, as well as pulling many of its more controversial time-saving microtransactions.

Read more

author: Eurogamer
text:
Vampire: The Masquerade - Bloodlines 2 development comes to an end with a big patch and some huge community-requested features

Vampire: The Masquerade - Bloodlines 2's final post-launch update is here, bringing a number of fixes and "community-requested" features, including photo mode, sniper sights, and dual-wield pistols.

Read more

author: Eurogamer
text:
Forza Horizon 6 players advised to apply new patch to avoid losing save data and progress

Playground Games is investigating an issue where "game save progress may be lost or reset" in Forza Horizon 6.

Read more

author: Eurogamer
text:
Microsoft reportedly considering restructuring Xbox into its own "wholly-owned subsidiary" and "moving faster" on releasing first-party games

A new report claims Microsoft is considering "spinning out" Xbox into its own company.

Read more

author: Eurogamer
text:
Send Crimson Desert your "fun and incredible videos", and you could win an all-expenses-paid trip to South Korea

Crimson Desert, the open-world action RPG from Pearl Abyss, has a new challenge for Greymanes – a community challenge event that invites you all to commemorate your time in Pywel by sharing a video.

Read more

author: Eurogamer
text:
Little Nightmares 3 – The Backstage wants you to meet The Puppeteer

Little Nightmares 3 – The Backstage – the DLC expansion announced during last week's Summer Game Fest festivities – is now available.

Read more

author: Eurogamer
text:
Spyro: A Realm Beyond exists only because of its "loud and consistent" community of fans

Spyro: A Realm Beyond exists only because of "loud and consistent" fans.
